text:
Jordan Chiles’ sister, Jazmin, says theUSgymnast has been subjected to ‘vile’ racism onsocial mediaafter she was forced to hand back the bronze medal she won at theParisOlympics.

Chiles, 23, finished third in the women’s floor final, with compatriot Simone Biles claiming the silver medal and Brazil’s Rebeca Andrade clinching gold.

But this only came after Chiles’ score of 13.666 was upgraded by 0.1 following an appeal from the American team at the Bercy Arena.

The change saw Romanian duo Ana Barbosu and Sabrina Voinea demoted to fourth and finish respectively after scoring 13.700.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris Olympics 2024

However, there was another shocking twist in the tale as the Court of Arbitration for Sport ruled that the judging panel had made an error in improving Chiles’ score given the appeal was issued outside of the one-minute window set by the International Gymnastics Federation.

It meant Chiles was pushed back to fifth spot, while Barbosu returned to the medal places – a decision which has left USA gymnastics ‘devastated’.

‘I am taking this time and removing myself from social media for my mental health. Thank you,’ Chiles later told her followers on Instagram.

While Chiles has since remained tight-lipped regarding the controversial ruling, her sister has been anything but – blasting the decision and urging fans to have her sibling in their prayers.

‘Please keep Jordan (and my family) in your prayers. Racism is real, it exists, it is alive and well,’ she wrote on Instagram.

‘They have officially, 5 days later, stripped her of one of her medals. Not because she didn’t win, not because she was drugged, not because she stepped out of bounds. Not because she wasn’t good enough.

‘But because the judges failed to give her difficulty and forced an inquiry to be made.

‘FOUR SECONDS. Her bronze was stripped over 4 seconds of time that would have never needed to happen if the judges did their job.

‘I love you baby sis. And I got yo back no matter what.’

She added: ‘Just so yall aware – in the HISTORY of the Olympics NO ONE has ever been stripped of their medal for this.

‘Also – there are only TWO ways you can be stripped of a medal. Cheating or doping.

‘She did neither.’

In a later post, Jazmin stressed that she did not think racism was the ‘root cause’ of her sister’s medal being removed.

‘And just to make sumn [sic] clear – the racism that’s BEHIND the comments and vulgar and vile comments that are being made toward my sister is what I’m speaking on,’ she continued.

‘I’m not calling them young ladies racist not am I saying racism is the root cause of this.

‘I’m saying it’s alive and well because in the last 5 days she has been called blacky. A monkey. Ni***r. And much more!! So keep yall little raggedy comments to yall self.

‘Just give the other girls a bronze and leave it at that! Period.’

On Saturday, the US Olympic Committee confirmed that they had appealed the decision and condemned the abuse that Chiles had faced on social media.

‘No athlete should be subject to such treatment,’ a statement tead.

‘We condemn the attacks and those who engage, support or instigate them.

‘We commend Jordan for conducting herself with integrity both on and off the competition floor, and we continue to stand by and support her.’

title: Jordan Chiles' sister condemns 'vile' racism after US gymnast is stripped of Olympic medal | Metro News
description: Jordan Chiles' sister says the US gymnast has been subjected to 'vile' racism after she was forced to hand back her Olympic bronze medal.
